I have everything I need. The project has:

1. **Direct command** (what CI runs): `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
2. **Pre-push hook**: `bin/pre-push` runs `bin/lint` plus `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` plus doc checks - so running `bin/pre-push` before pushing will run clippy
3. **CI script**: `ci/test/lint-clippy.sh` which just runs the same cargo clippy command

So the user can:
1. Run `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` from the repo root (matches CI)
2. Run `bin/pre-push` before pushing (runs lint + clippy + doc checks)
3. Run `ci/test/lint-clippy.sh` (same as #1 but via the CI script)

I'll give a clear, concise answer.
</details></think>

---


Here’s how to run the same Clippy checks as CI on your commits locally:

## Run Clippy (matches CI)

From the repo root:

```bash
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
```

That’s what CI uses (see `ci/test/lint-clippy.sh`).

## Run full pre-push checks (lint + Clippy + docs)

Before pushing, you can run:

```bash
bin/pre-push
```

That runs:

1. `bin/lint` (formatting and other lints)
2. `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
3. `bin/doc --no-deps` and `bin/doc --document-private-items`

So `bin/pre-push` is the single command that approximates what will run in CI for your branch.

## Optional: pre-push git hook

To run these checks automatically before every push, you can install the script as a hook:

```bash
ln -sf ../../bin/pre-push .git/hooks/pre-push
```

Then `git push` will run the same checks first.

---
